skill.name: when-building-backend-api-orchestrate-api-development
skill.description: Guides production REST API development from requirements to deployment using a five-phase, test-driven workflow with specialist agent coordination.
skill.summary: Orchestrate a production REST API build with TDD, quality gates, documentation, and blue-green deployment.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many external command, network, filesystem, and environment-access patterns, but review shows they are primarily markdown workflow examples rather than hidden executable code. The skill is not blocked, but it should publish with a medium-risk warning because users or agents may copy shell, kubectl, npm, curl, and deployment snippets into real environments. The weak-cryptography and critical exfiltration heuristics appear to be false positives because no malicious intent or data exfiltration behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: Operator-Executed External Command Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill contains extensive bash, npm, npx claude-flow, kubectl, curl, and deployment snippets. They are documented workflow examples, not hidden installer code, but they can change infrastructure or production traffic if copied and run without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are clearly present and include production deployment operations. Confidence is below very high because they are markdown examples within an SOP, not automatically executed code.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Shell Script Parameters in Examples
medium_findings.description: Example shell scripts accept API names, endpoint paths, and versions as positional parameters and interpolate them into memory keys, file paths, test commands, and deployment messages. This is legitimate automation guidance, but real implementations should validate or quote inputs carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interpolation is visible in example scripts, but no direct malicious payload or automatic execution path was found. The risk depends on whether users adapt these snippets without validation.

low_findings.title: Environment Secret Access in Example API Code
low_findings.description: The sample registration handler reads process.env.JWT_SECRET to sign a JWT. This is a normal backend pattern, but users should validate secret presence and avoid logging or exposing token material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment access is directly shown and is semantically tied to JWT signing. It appears legitimate and no network exfiltration of the secret was found.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Network Calls in Examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes API test calls and placeholder curl requests to example domains. These support testing and monitoring examples and do not show credential exfiltration, but real URLs should be reviewed before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network patterns are visible, but the domains are examples and no sensitive data is sent in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Temporary File Export Path in Handoff Example
low_findings.description: The workflow suggests exporting a workflow JSON file under /tmp using an API identifier. This is a minor filesystem concern if adapted without safe file naming or cleanup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem path is explicit and limited to an example export. No evidence of unauthorized file reads or persistence was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Heuristic: Commands Plus Network Plus Credentials Reviewed
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner combined command examples, network examples, and environment variable access into a critical heuristic. Human review found no hidden executable file, prompt injection, malicious endpoint, or credential exfiltration pattern, so this is downgraded to medium operational risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The ingredients for the heuristic are present, but the surrounding context shows documentation and examples. I did not find evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner reported weak cryptography across SKILL.md and the DOT diagram, but the reviewed context contains workflow text, TDD wording, labels, and ordinary JWT or bcrypt examples rather than MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Several cited lines clearly contain descriptions or diagram labels, not weak crypto calls. The only cryptographic code reviewed uses bcrypt hashing and JWT signing in an example.

content.value_statement: Building a production REST API requires coordinated planning, testing, documentation, and deployment work. This skill provides a phased workflow for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to guide specialist agents through that process.
actual_capabilities: Defines a five-phase REST API delivery workflow from requirements through production monitoring.
Coordinates planning, architecture, database, QA, backend, security, documentation, and DevOps agent roles.
Provides TDD cycle guidance for writing tests, implementing endpoints, and reviewing code quality.
Includes quality gates for test coverage, security review, performance validation, and deployment readiness.
Documents blue-green deployment, rollback, monitoring, and handoff steps for production API releases.
limitations: Does not include executable project source code or reusable automation files.
Assumes claude-flow, Node.js tooling, Kubernetes, and related DevOps tools are available when examples are used.
Requires human review before running deployment, kubectl, npm, curl, or shell examples in real environments.
Uses generic API examples that must be adapted to each organization, stack, and compliance requirement.

use_cases.title: Plan a New REST API
use_cases.description: Turn product requirements into endpoint definitions, architecture decisions, database design, and test strategy before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Coordinate TDD Implementation
use_cases.description: Use red, green, and refactor cycles to build endpoints with tests, coverage targets, and code review checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Release
use_cases.description: Validate security, performance, documentation, rollback planning, blue-green deployment, and monitoring before launch.

prompt_templates.title: Start an API Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to plan a production REST API for [product]. Identify phases, agents, deliverables, and quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Design API Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the planning and architecture phases to design endpoints, data models, authentication, OpenAPI structure, and database requirements for [API name].

prompt_templates.title: Run a TDD Endpoint Cycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TDD phase to build [endpoint]. Create test goals first, then implementation tasks, refactor checks, and completion criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Release Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing and deployment phases to assess release readiness for [API name]. Include security, performance, documentation, rollback, and monitoring checks.

output_examples.input: Plan a user management API with authentication and profile endpoints.
output: A phased delivery plan with product, architecture, database, QA, backend, security, documentation, and DevOps responsibilities.
A list of required endpoints, test coverage targets, OpenAPI deliverables, deployment gates, and monitoring expectations.

output_examples.input: Prepare the production readiness review for an order service API.
output: A release checklist covering passing tests, security audit status, performance benchmarks, documentation, rollback readiness, and monitoring dashboards.
A go or no-go decision structure with blockers, required fixes, and post-deployment validation steps.

output_examples.input: Coordinate TDD for a registration endpoint.
output: A red, green, and refactor sequence with test scenarios, implementation tasks, review criteria, and memory handoff points.
A completion definition based on passing tests, coverage, validation behavior, and code review approval.

best_practices: Review and adapt every shell, deployment, and network command before execution.
Keep the TDD gates strict: write failing tests before implementation and verify coverage after each endpoint.
Store phase outputs consistently so later agents can trace requirements, architecture, tests, and deployment decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not run deployment examples against production without environment review and approval.
Do not treat placeholder URLs, secrets, or API names as ready for real systems.
Do not skip security, performance, documentation, or rollback gates to shorten the release timeline.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps plan and coordinate a production-ready REST API workflow from requirements through monitoring.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete API project?
faq.answer: No. It provides orchestration guidance, example commands, phase outputs, and quality gates.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this suitable for regulated APIs?
faq.answer: It includes security and compliance planning prompts, but domain experts must validate regulated requirements.

faq.question: Are the included commands safe to run directly?
faq.answer: They are examples and workflow snippets. Review environment, permissions, and inputs before running them.

faq.question: What teams benefit most from it?
faq.answer: Backend, QA, security, documentation, and DevOps teams can use it to coordinate API delivery work.
